solution:
```cpp
#include <vector>
#include <stack>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    vector<int> maxDepthAfterSplit(string seq) {
        vector<int> result(seq.size());
        stack<int> stk;
        int a_depth = 0, b_depth = 0;
        
        for (int i = 0; i < seq.size(); ++i) {
            if (seq[i] == '(') {
                if (a_depth <= b_depth) {
                    stk.push(0);
                    a_depth++;
                } else {
                    stk.push(1);
                    b_depth++;
                }
            } else {
                int assign = stk.top();
                stk.pop();
                result[i] = assign;
                if (assign == 0) {
                    a_depth--;
                } else {
                    b_depth--;
                }
            }
        }
        return result;
    }
};
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a greedy approach to distribute parentheses between two subsequences, ensuring both remain valid and minimizing the maximum nesting depth. Each character is processed in constant time, resulting in linear time complexity. The space complexity is linear due to the stack used to track assignments.
